insights.recommendation: Rebalance panels by moving 12-15 from Sam Rivera to Casey Kim and Morgan Chen immediately.

evidence.key_insight: Sam Rivera is significantly overloaded with 78 panels, above the safe maximum of 65.

alternative.trade_off: Higher cost for more detailed analysis and recommendations.

insights.rag_context: [{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Interviewer overload risk assessment. Industry benchmark: healthy interviewer load is 4-6 interviews per week, or 15-25 per month. Over a hiring cycle, 40-50 total panels is considered sustainable without calibration fatigue. Sam Rivera at 77 panels is 54-93% above safe maximum — high risk of scoring inconsistency. Jamie Osei at 64 is at the upper threshold. Casey Kim and Morgan Chen at 55 are within healthy range. Recommendation: cap any single interviewer at 65 panels per cycle; rebalance Sam Rivera immediately.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"panel_load_balancer\",\"category\":\"overload_risk\"},\"id\":\"panel-002\"}"},{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Interviewer panel load distribution (10 interviewers, 200 candidates). Sam Rivera: 77 panels (highest load, 38.5% of candidates). Jamie Osei: 64 panels. Riley Patel: 61. Alex Wang: 61. Drew Singh: 61. Taylor Brooks: 59. Quinn Diaz: 57. Jordan Lee: 56. Morgan Chen: 55. Casey Kim: 55. Average load: 60.6 panels per interviewer. Standard deviation: ~6.5. Sam Rivera is a significant outlier at 27% above average — risk of burnout and inconsistent scoring.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"panel_load_balancer\",\"category\":\"interviewer_load_summary\"},\"id\":\"panel-001\"}"},{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Interview scoring consistency and panel quality benchmarks. Average cumulative round score across all 200 candidates: calculated from data. Score variance increases with interviewer fatigue — overloaded interviewers show wider score ranges. Industry standard: inter-rater reliability (IRR) should be above 0.7 (Cohen's kappa) for technical roles. Best practice: run calibration sessions every 30 panels per interviewer. Panels with 3+ interviewers have better IRR than 1-2 person panels. Recommendation: require minimum 2 interviewers per panel for all senior and above roles.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"panel_load_balancer\",\"category\":\"scoring_consistency\"},\"id\":\"panel-005\"}"},{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Panel rebalancing strategy for overloaded interviewers. To rebalance: move 12-15 panels from Sam Rivera to Casey Kim and Morgan Chen (capacity available). Rotate Quinn Diaz (57) and Jordan Lee (56) into senior-level panels they haven't covered yet. Establish a panel rotation policy: no interviewer should be on more than 3 panels per week. Add a scheduling rule in ATS: flag any interviewer exceeding 70% of average panel load. Cross-train interviewers across role types to improve rebalancing flexibility.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"panel_load_balancer\",\"category\":\"rebalancing_strategy\"},\"id\":\"panel-003\"}"}]

evaluation.reasoning: The recommendation is actionable and well-grounded in the RAG context. It aligns perfectly with the golden scenario, addressing the overload issue with Sam Rivera.

insights.recommendation: Investigate team culture and speed up the offer process to improve acceptance rates.

evidence.key_insight: Team culture concerns account for 26% of declines compared to the 8-12% benchmark, and 39% of declines are related to offer process speed.

alternative.trade_off: Lower cost but with potentially less nuanced understanding of context.

insights.rag_context: [{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Offer outcomes summary (57 offers made, 200 candidates total). Offers accepted: 34 (59.6% acceptance rate). Offers declined: 23 (40.4% decline rate). Overall pipeline conversion: 34 hires from 200 candidates = 17% end-to-end conversion. Industry benchmark for offer acceptance: 70-80% for senior engineering roles. Current 59.6% acceptance rate is 10-20 points below benchmark — significant gap requiring action. Each declined offer represents ~60 hours of interview time wasted across panels.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"offer_insights\",\"category\":\"offer_acceptance_summary\"},\"id\":\"offer-001\"}"},{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Offer acceptance rates by sourcing channel. Company Website converts best once an offer is made: 85.7% acceptance rate (6 of 7 offers). Glassdoor is second at 77.8% (7 of 9). AngelList and LinkedIn both sit at 50.0%. Hackathon is the weakest closer at 40.0% — candidates from events may be passively exploring. Benchmark: industry average offer acceptance is 70-75%; channels below 55% warrant re-evaluation.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"sourcing_quality\",\"category\":\"channel_acceptance\"},\"id\":\"src-002\"}"},{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Industry benchmarks for rejection patterns in technical hiring. Typical rejection rates: Engineering roles 55-65%, Data Science 50-60%, Product 45-55%. Expected early-stage (R1) rejection rate: 35-45% — screens out unqualified applicants. Expected compensation mismatch rate: 5-10% — above 12% signals JD salary range not being shared. Background check failure rate benchmark: 2-4%; above 8% suggests insufficient pre-screening. Culture fit rejection rate: 5-10%; above 12% may signal inconsistent culture definition across panels. No-show benchmark: under 5%; above 7% suggests poor candidate engagement or scheduling friction.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"rejection_pattern\",\"category\":\"industry_rejection_benchmarks\"},\"id\":\"rej-004\"}"},{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Offer decline reason breakdown (23 declines). Team culture concerns: 6 (26.1%) — largest category, suggests culture evaluation gap. Competing offer: 3 (13.0%). Timeline too long: 3 (13.0%). Role scope mismatch: 3 (13.0%). Counter-offer accepted: 3 (13.0%). Compensation too low: 2 (8.7%). Personal reasons: 2 (8.7%). Relocation required: 1 (4.3%). Team culture concerns at 26% is unusually high — benchmark is 8-12%. Combined timeline + competing offer + counter-offer = 39% of declines are speed-related.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"offer_insights\",\"category\":\"decline_reasons\"},\"id\":\"offer-002\"}"}]

evaluation.reasoning: The insight is actionable and relevant, with strong grounding. It aligns well with the golden scenario, highlighting culture and process speed issues.
